texto: It's peculiar to me,' she said, 'that everybody pays so much attention to living and so little to dying. Why are these high-powered scientists always screwing around trying to prolong life instead of finding pleasant ways to end it? There must be a hell of a lot of people in the world like me--who want to die but haven't got the guts.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Horace McCoy: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “It's peculiar to me,' she said, 'that everybody pays so much attention to living and so little to…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, dying, euthanasia pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e euthanasia.

frase_original: It's peculiar to me,' she said, 'that everybody pays so much attention to living and so little to dying. Why are these high-powered scientists always screwing around trying to prolong life instead of finding pleasant ways to end it? There must be a hell of a lot of people in the world like me--who want to die but haven't got the guts.
